title: Open hole positioning editor directly on the OcarinaVisual model

requirements.text: Add a clearly visible 'Position Holes' button directly on or immediately below the OcarinaVisual play-along component in the main App layout, so users can access hole positioning without navigating into the Settings modal.

quotes: Let me set the holes configuration/ positioning on the model

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Position Holes' button is visible in the main interface near the OcarinaVisual component.
The button is accessible without opening the Settings modal.
Clicking the button opens the HolePositioningEditor component.

requirements.text: When the 'Position Holes' button is clicked, open the HolePositioningEditor component as a modal or inline overlay directly on the OcarinaVisual component, so the user can drag and reposition each of the 4 holes (left index, left middle, right index, right middle) on the current ocarina background (stock photo or uploaded photo) without leaving the main view.

quotes: Let me set the holes configuration/ positioning on the model

acceptanceCriteria: The HolePositioningEditor opens overlaying or directly adjacent to the OcarinaVisual component.
The editor shows the same background image currently used by OcarinaVisual (stock image or user-uploaded photo).
All 4 holes are draggable (mouse and touch) within the editor.
A Save/Set button confirms and persists the new hole positions via the useCustomHolePositions hook.
A Cancel or Close button discards changes and closes the editor.
After saving, the OcarinaVisual component immediately reflects the updated hole positions.

requirements.text: Ensure the HolePositioningEditor opened from the OcarinaVisual context uses the stock grey ocarina image as the background by default when no user photo has been uploaded, matching the background currently displayed by OcarinaVisual, so the user can visually align holes to the actual image.

quotes: Let me set the holes configuration/ positioning on the model

acceptanceCriteria: When no user photo is uploaded, the HolePositioningEditor shows the stock ocarina image as background.
When a user photo is uploaded, the HolePositioningEditor shows the user photo as background.
The hole overlay markers are positioned using the current values from useCustomHolePositions.

constraints: Do not break or modify the existing HolePositioningEditor component's drag-and-drop logic (mouse + touch support) or Save/Reset functionality.
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
useCustomHolePositions hook must remain the single source of truth for hole position state and persistence.
nonGoals: Changing the fingering pattern (open/closed state) of holes — that is handled by FingeringEditor.
Modifying Settings modal layout or other settings sections.
Adding new hole positioning features beyond drag-and-drop repositioning.
